violations.text: Potatoes were observed in the handwashing sink at time of inspection. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Establishment is vacuum packaging soupy without a HACCP plan. HACCP plan is required. Soupy was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Milk, cream, and oat milk were observed held at 48-63 degrees Fahrenheit on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been on the counter for 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Potato salad was placed hot in the deli display cooler in containers greater than four inches deep with the lid on. Proper cooling methods were reviewed with the person in charge during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025
Pan of chili observed cooling in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

Pasta salad packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address.PIC informed of proper labeling procedures New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023
